Short title and commencement:- (1) These regulations may be called the National Highways Authority of India (Recruitment, Seniority and Promotion) Second Amendment Regulations, 2016. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In regulation 3 of the National Highways Authority of India (Recruitment, Seniority and Promotion) Regulations, 1996 (hereinafter referred to as the principal regulations), after clause (j), the following clauses shall be inserted, namely:- “(k) “Departmental Promotion Committee” means a Committee constituted by the Appointing Authority for judging the suitability of officers and employees of the Authority for making promotions to higher grade(s)/post(s), as specified in Regulation 13B. (l) “Executive Committee” means a Committee comprising of Chairman and full-time Members of the Authority.” 3. In regulation 5 of the principal regulations, for sub-regulation (1), the following sub-regulation shall be substituted, namely:- “(1) The name of the post to which this Chapter applies, its number, classification and scale of pay attached thereto shall be as specified in columns 1 to 4 of the Schedule for each category of posts, provided that sanctioned number of posts upto the level of General Manager may be increased or decreased with the approval of Chairman and such an exercise to review the sanctioned strength shall be undertaken every year and promulgated by an administrative order.” 4. In regulation 10 of the principal regulations, for sub-regulation (1), the following sub-regulation shall be substituted, namely:- “Age of superannuation – (1) All officers and employees (regular as well as officers and employees appointed on contract) shall superannuate from service /cease to be in service (as the case may be) at the age, as applicable to the Central Government employees.” 5. In regulation 12 of the principal regulations, for sub-regulation (1), the following sub-regulation shall be substituted, namely:- “(1) All vacancies in the Authority to be filled up by promotion up to the level of Manager & equivalent grades shall be filled through Departmental Promotion Committee procedure as per Regulation 13B. All other vacancies in the Authority shall be circulated among eligible Officers, and employees and in addition notified in the following manner, namely:- (a) A detailed advertisement shall be hosted on the website of the Authority; and (b) An indicative advertisement shall be published in one Hindi, one English daily newspaper and the Employment News.” 6. In regulation 12 of the principal regulations, for sub-regulation (4), the following sub- regulation shall be substituted, namely:- “(4) The crucial date for determination of eligibility for posts to be filled on direct recruitment/ direct recruitment through lateral entry/ deputation / contract basis shall be the last date prescribed for the receipt of applications.” -- 60 of 112 -- ¹Hkkx IIIμ[k.M 4º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 61 7. In regulation 12 of the principal regulations, after sub-regulation (5), the following clause shall be inserted, namely:- “(a) Upto 20% of sanctioned strength of Technical Cadre shall be filled from Central Engineering Service (Roads) cadre and the balance 80% should be met by 50% (maximum) from permanent cadre of the Authority and 30% by deputation from other services. In case of shortfall in any of above modes, the shortfall may be met from either of the other modes.” 8. In regulation 13 of the principal regulations, for sub-regulation (3), the following sub-regulation shall be substituted, namely:- “(3) Appointment by absorption including existing cadre of NHAI officers / employees, should not exceed 50% of the sanctioned posts of Technical Cadre at the levels of Manager, Deputy General Manager and General Manager at any point of time; and the absorption shall be undertaken in a phased manner enhancing the recruitment from 25% to 50% in the coming recruitment years.” 9. After regulations 13A and 13A(1) of the principal regulations, the following regulations shall be inserted, namely:- “13B. Departmental Promotion Committee (D.P.C.): (1) There shall be Departmental Promotion Committee(s) for selection of candidates by promotion for the posts upto the level of Manager in the Authority. The composition of the Departmental Promotion Committee(s) shall be same as prescribed under Regulation 11. (2) Frequency at which DPC should meet: The DPC shall meet once in every year in the month of January. The vacancies as on 1st Day of January each year shall be considered by DPC for making promotion. (3) Date for determining eligibility: The date for determining the eligibility of Officers/employees for promotion shall be the first day of the January of the year in which the Officer/Employee is being considered for promotion. (4) Guidelines for DPCs: The DPC should assess the suitability of officers/employees for promotion on the basis of their Service Records and with particular reference to the APARs/CRs for five preceding financial years. If one or more APARs/CRs have not been written for any reason during the relevant period, the DPC should consider the APARs/CRs of the years preceding the period in question. (5) The DPC shall determine the merit of those bein g assessed for promotion with reference to the following benchmark and accordingly grade the Officer/employee as “fit” or “unfit” only:- For promotion to the posts having scale : “Good” of pay upto PB-3 with GP 6600/- (6) Only those Officers/employees who are graded “f it” (i.e. who meet the prescribed benchmark) by the DPC shall be included and arranged in the select panel in order of their inter-se-seniority in the feeder grade. Those Officers/ employees who are graded “unfit” (in terms of prescribed benchmark) by the DPC shall not be included in the select panel. Thus there shall be no supersession in promotion among those who are graded “fit” (in terms of the prescribed benchmark) by the DPC. (7) The proceedings of the DPC shall be legally valid and shall be acted upon not-with- standing the absence of any of its members other than the Presiding Officer, provided that the member was duly invited but he absented himself for one reason or the other and there was no deliberate attempt to exclude him from the deliberation of the DPC and provided further that the majority of the members constituting the DPC are present in the meeting. -- 61 of 112 -- 62 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART III —SEC. 4] (8) Implementation of the recommendations of DPC: T he recommendations of the DPC should be duly approved by the Appointing Authority and a clearance from the Vigilance Division of the Authority shall also be obtained before making promotion. (9) The panel of selected candidates shall remain operative for one year from the date of approval of the Appointing Authority. (10) Action on issues which are not specifically co vered in above sub regulations shall be taken as per guidelines/instructions issued by the Central Government in this regard from time to time.” 13C. Appointment on Contract: (1) Criteria for appointment: Chairman may engage officers on “Contract basis” where the age of superannuation in their parent department is less than the age of superannuation prescribed in Central Government provided that such officer has completed at least 2 years continuous service on deputation with the Authority. (2) Period of engagement: The period for which such Officers could be engaged on “Contract basis” would be initially one year and may be extended on yearly basis or curtailed subject to satisfactory performance provided that the period of such engagement shall not be more than three years or shall not go beyond the date of superannuation prescribed by the Central Government, whichever is earlier.” 10. In the Schedule to the principal regulations, the following note shall be substituted, namely:- “Note 1:- Wherever the deputation is prescribed as a mode of recruitment in Column 5 of the Schedules, the period of deputation prescribed in Column 8 thereof shall be substituted, namely:- Period of deputation:- Initial period of deputation shall be 03 years, which may be extended for a period of 02 years with the approval of Chairman. Further extension in the period of deputation for another period of 2 years beyond 05 years shall be done with the approval of Ministry of Road Transport & Highways, subject to satisfactory performance.” 11. In the Schedule to the principal regulations, the Schedules prescribing the eligibility criteria and qualifications for the following posts shall be deleted, namely: “General Manager (Private Investment) Deputy General Manager (Electronics) Manager (Electronics) Assistant Manager (Tech.) Assistant Manager (Vigilance) Jamadar Peon” 12. In the Schedule to the principal regulations, the Schedules prescribing the eligibility criteria and qualifications for the following posts shall be inserted, namely: “Deputy General Manager (Media Relations) Deputy Manager (Information Technology) Deputy Manager (Vigilance) Assistant Manager (Legal) Senior Accountant Senior Principal Private Secretary Principal Private Secretary -- 62 of 112 -- ¹Hkkx IIIμ[k.M 4º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 63 Manager (Environment) Manager (Rajbhasha) Hindi Translator Senior Librarian & Information Officer Librarian Library & Information assistant Administrative Assistant (Library) Parliament Assistant Protocol Assistant Senior Receptionist Senior Record Keeper Record Keeper Administrative Assistant (Stores) Senior Multi Tasking Staff Multi Tasking Staff 13.
